description.en: A large crocodilian endemic to the southeastern United States, known for its broad, rounded snout.
description.zh: 美国东南部特有的大型鳄类，以其宽阔圆润的吻部而闻名。

anatomy.en: American alligators are living tanks that have survived since the time of the dinosaurs! They are dark almost-black reptiles that can grow up to 15 feet long. You can tell them apart from crocodiles by their nose: alligators have a wide, U-shaped snout, and when their mouth is closed, you can't see their bottom teeth.
anatomy.zh: 美洲短吻鳄是从恐龙时代存活至今的活体坦克！它们是深色、几乎是黑色的爬行动物，体长可达15英尺。你可以通过它们的鼻子把它们和鳄鱼区分开来：短吻鳄有宽阔的U形吻部，而且当它们闭上嘴时，你是看不到它们下排牙齿的。

ecology_and_behavior.en: These gators are excellent ecosystem engineers. They use their tails and snouts to dig deep holes called 'gator holes' that hold water during dry seasons, providing a lifeline for fish, turtles, and birds. You can often see them lying completely still on the muddy banks with their mouths wide open to cool down in the hot sun.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 这些短吻鳄是出色的生态系统工程师。它们用尾巴和鼻子挖出被称为‘鳄鱼坑’的深坑，在旱季这些坑里会蓄水，为鱼类、海龟和鸟类提供生命线。你经常可以看到它们完全一动不动地躺在泥泞的河岸上，张开大嘴在烈日下乘凉。

habitat_and_distribution.en: They live exclusively in the southeastern United States, especially in Florida and Louisiana. They love freshwater habitats like slow-moving rivers, swamps, marshes, and lakes. Sometimes they even show up in people's swimming pools or on golf courses!
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们只生活在美国东南部，特别是在佛罗里达州和路易斯安那州。它们喜欢淡水栖息地，比如水流缓慢的河流、沼泽、湿地和湖泊。有时它们甚至会出现在人们的游泳池或高尔夫球场上！

habitat.text_en: Freshwater swamps, marshes, and rivers in the southeastern US
habitat.text_zh: 美国东南部的淡水沼泽、湿地和河流
